Defendant Soco West, Inc.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-19-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Charles P. Murrin, Esq., of MURRIN & ASSOCIATES LLC (925-284-5770), appearing for defendant / moving party Soco West, Inc.
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Gilbert Forbes

RULING:
DEFENDANT SOCO WEST, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Soco West, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. By failing to demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery on plaintiff seeking all facts, witnesses and documents supporting plaintiff's contentions against it, and that plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden as to collateral estoppel. Since defendant did not attach responses to contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant did not demonstrate what plaintiff's claims are as to it and thus did not demonstrate that any of the collateral estoppel requirements have been met. Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare an order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tamara Willat, CSR # 4609 = (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-10-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Robert C. Crane, Esq., and Brian h. Buddell, Esq., of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party Union Carbide Corporation
James P. Nevin, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Gilbert Forbes

RULING:
DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Union Carbide Corporation's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos supplied by defendant. Defendant's hearsay objections to the 3M documents are sustained. The 3M custodian was unable to provide any information about the person(s) who either wrote or provided the information about defendant's asserted supply of asbestos to 3M's facility, much less that such person(s) had knowledge of the relevant facts from personal observation. (Jazayeri v. Mao (2009) 174 Cal. App. 4th 301, 322 ('The key to establishing the admissibility of a document made in the regular course of business is proof that the person who wrote the information or provided it had knowledge of the facts from personal observation.')). Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
